label: Cost Per Serving
description: Calculator that uses recipe quantities, batch yield, and storehouse prices to compute cost per serving, cost per batch, and highlight hair-supportive foods for Black hair nutrition planning.

recipeContext.description: Base recipe and scaled recipe context: name, domain, baseline servings, scaled servings.

batchYield.description: Optional batch yield summary used to normalize cost per serving across batch sizes.

ingredientLines.description: Ingredients with quantities, units, and price-per-unit from the price book or manual entry.

hairNutritionProfile.description: Optional per-ingredient hair-support tags and scores for Black hair nutrition analysis.

currency.description: Currency code for all cost outputs.

prefs.description: User preferences for rounding, cost emphasis, and highlighting of Black hair nutrition foods.

totals.description: Overall cost totals including costPerServing, costPerBatch, and costPerDay if a daily plan is available.

perIngredientCosts.description: Cost share per ingredient, including any hair nutrition-related tags or notes.

hairNutritionCosts.description: Subset and summary for ingredients that support Black hair nutrition, with per-serving and per-period costs.

meta.description: Metadata including calculatorVersion, runContext, and warning messages.

shim.description: Pure-logic shim that accepts validated input and returns cost-per-serving outputs. Designed to be invoked from React hooks, workers, or SessionRunner steps without UI coupling.

stepTemplates.title: Review cost per serving
stepTemplates.desc: Look over the total cost per serving and per batch. Adjust your scaled servings if the cost feels too high for your budget.

metadata.cueNotes: Use this moment to decide whether to scale the recipe up or down for your household and budget.

stepTemplates.title: Check Black hair nutrition foods
stepTemplates.desc: Review which ingredients support hair and scalp health, and confirm you want to keep or enhance them in this meal plan.

metadata.cueNotes: Prioritize hair-supportive foods even if they cost a bit more, when your budget allows.
